sourceMovie.title: Ex Machina
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ex Machina works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ex Machina clicked for you, Her (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Blade Runner 2049
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ex Machina clicked for you, Blade Runner 2049 (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ex Machina?
faq.answer: Try Her, Blade Runner 2049, A.I. Artificial Intelligence—each shares DNA with Ex Machina while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ex Machina?
faq.answer: Ex Machina (2015) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ex Machina worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
